route_tested: opaque-append OR canonical-store + owned-tile reads persistent cache (default-off KV_OPAQUE_READ; reverted)

copy_removed.note: when KV_OPAQUE fires (decode capture-ctx>=2048), the full-MAXC copy E_49152 is ABSENT from the captured graph and the owned tile fires -> the copy IS removed.

first_decode_step_correct.note: the FIRST decode step (the captured step) is byte-identical to baseline -> the copy-free opaque read is value-correct for one step.

multi_step_replay_garbage.note: replays (start_pos 2051+) produce 151936 (out-of-range/NaN) -> cross-replay KV persistence is LOST once the copy is removed.

owned_tile_ctx_restricted.note: the owned tile (the only viable opaque read) is correct ONLY at ctx>=2048 (its ctx-gate is for CORRECTNESS, not just perf -- S=48 over-splits short KV to NaN). So KV_OPAQUE cannot apply at ctx<2048.

in_model_findings.jit_capture_ctx_subtlety: a single prefill+decode TinyJit captures at the prefill (short) ctx -> the captured graph is canonical and KV_OPAQUE never fires; only a fresh decode-jit captured at ctx>=2048 fires it.

root_cause: the canonical `assigned_kv = Tensor(cache_kv.after(store))` materialization provides BOTH the full-MAXC copy AND the @function(precompile=True) cross-replay persistence linkage (it makes cache_kv a tracked stateful in/out). Removing the copy (reading un-materialized cache halves after the store, or the opaque append + repoint/no-repoint) breaks that persistence: repoint -> REDUCE read-after-write hazard (crash); no-repoint/un-materialized -> KV does not persist across JIT replays (garbage after step 1). The copy and cross-replay persistence are COUPLED in the functional model.
